items.title: Prologis Makes £14 Billion Best and Final Offer for UK’s Segro
items.description: Prologis Inc. has submitted a best and final offer for Segro Plc that values the UK’s largest publicly traded landlord at about £14 billion ($18.7 billion).

items.title: Kuwait Set to Issue Dollar Bonds as Iran Strikes Batter Country
items.description: Kuwait, under daily missile and drone attacks from Iran over the past two weeks, is aiming to sell dollar bonds on Wednesday.

items.title: UK Stocks an Underappreciated AI Play, Brooks Macdonald CIO Says
items.description: The UK is an underappreciated knowledge-sector superpower and remains a “happy zone” for active stock-picking, according to William Hobbs, chief investment officer at Brooks Macdonald Asset Management

items.title: Alphabet Earnings Need to Show Cloud Growth as AI Spending Soars
items.description: With AI spending skyrocketing, investors are looking for Alphabet Inc.’s earnings to show strong growth in its cloud-computing business, which would demonstrate a clear return on the company’s investm

items.title: Funds’ Bullish Wagers on European Gas Jump the Most Since War
items.description: Investment funds and other speculators boosted their net-long positions in European gas by the most since the first week of the Iran war, signaling a strong conviction that prices still have room to r

items.title: MSEDCL Said to Pick Banks for India IPO Worth Up to $1 Billion
items.description: Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Co., the power distribution company in India’s richest state, has shortlisted six investment banks for a planned initial public offering that could raise bet

items.title: TPG Eyes Sale to Japan Hotel REIT for $860 Million
items.description: Investment fund TPG Asia Real Estate plans to sell a Tokyo hotel property to Japan Hotel REIT Investment Co. as a tourism boom unfolds and boosts valuations, people familiar with the matter said.

items.title: JPY/USD: Why Is Japan Yen So Weak? Can Government Intervention Help Currency?
items.description: The yen’s weakness has become a growing issue for Japan’s policymakers, given its role in driving up import prices and household living costs.

items.title: Middle East Shipping Threats Rise as Owners Plot Best Course
items.description: Challenges to shipping in the Middle East escalated, as a call from Iran-backed Houthi rebels against servicing Saudi ports spurred unusual maneuvers in the Red Sea, while a European Union naval group

items.title: FTSE 100 Live: UK Inflation Slows More Than Expected in June
Rare Earths Giant Lynas Shares Fall to Five-Month Low After Revenue Miss
items.description: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd. shares dropped to a five-month low after Australia’s largest rare-earths producer reported quarterly sales that missed analysts’ expectations.

items.title: South African Deputy Finance Chief Faces Potential PIC Ouster
items.description: South Africa’s deputy finance minister said he’ll submit a formal response defending his position as chairman of the Public Investment Corp. when its board meets next week to discuss recent turmoil at

items.title: Copper Wavers Near $14,000 as Traders Watch for Trump’s Tariff
items.description: Copper pulled back from its highest level since early June as traders watched for any announcement from the White House on its plans for import tariffs on the metal.

items.title: PHP/USD: Philippines Joins India in Stepping Currency Defense as Oil Rises
items.description: The Philippine central bank intervened in the foreign-exchange market as the peso fell to a record low, joining India in protecting currencies hit by surging oil prices.

items.title: Thai Bonds Are Beating Asia and Analysts Predict More Gains
items.description: Thailand’s bonds have beaten their emerging Asian peers over the past month and analysts predict they will extend gains on favorable supply dynamics and slower inflation.

items.title: Gold Extends Gains as Dip-Buyers Shrug Off US-Iran Escalation
items.description: Gold extended gains, supported by dip-buyers, despite escalating Middle East tensions.

items.title: Yen Rebounds From Four-Decade Low as BOJ May Hike Rates Faster
items.description: The yen weakened past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986 before recovering some ground on the prospect of faster interest-rate hikes from the Bank of Japan.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
items.description: Oil jumped above $95, extending this month’s price jump to 30%, as the US and Iran played down the prospect of talks and disruptions to global supplies continued to mount.

items.title: Stocks Fall Ahead of Alphabet as Brent Hits $95: Markets Wrap
items.description: US stocks fell ahead of Alphabet Inc.’s earnings as traders waited for the clearest read yet on whether the vast sums hyperscalers are pouring into artificial intelligence will pay off. Brent topped $

items.title: Indian Refiners Halt Iraq Oil Loadings on Rising Hormuz Risk
